title: lithocarpus pulongtauensis
intro: Lithocarpus pulongtauensis, also known as the Philippine white oak, is a plant species belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Lithocarpus pulongtauensis has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Lithocarpus pulongtauensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
